title: myoarm-lambda-ep: λ-EP controller for the MyoSuite myoArmReachRandom-v0 environment
description: Reference implementation of a biologically motivated reach controller for the MyoSuite myoArmReachRandom-v0 environment (20-DoF, 34 Hill-type muscles). Combines Feldman's λ-equilibrium-point hypothesis, a minimum-jerk virtual trajectory, a 200 ms visuomotor correction, and γ-compatible spinal reflexes (Ia, Ib, reciprocal inhibition). Includes ablation experiments (F3–F17) and the figure-generation pipeline behind the bioRxiv pre-print 'Decoupling smoothness, accuracy, and kinematic invariance in biological reach: an ablation study of an equilibrium-point controller in a 34-muscle arm model'.

creators.name: Kobayashi, Jun
creators.affiliation: Department of Intelligent and Control Systems, Graduate School of Computer Science and Systems Engineering, Kyushu Institute of Technology, Iizuka, Fukuoka, Japan

notes: The related_identifiers DOI is the bioRxiv pre-print and will be filled in after submission. The software is the reference implementation for the manuscript.
